title: Dephosphorylation of 5´-ends of DNA using CIP (M0290)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bgqa4","text":"Protocol for Dephosphorylation of 5´-ends of DNA using CIP in Restriction Enzyme Reaction. Uses the Calf Intestinal Alkaline Phosphatase  (CIP - M0290).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dv71s","text":"Dephosphorylation of 5' -ends of DNA in Restriction Enzyme Reaction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":67}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5g534","text":"The phosphate can be added directly into the digestion reaction during or after DNA digestion","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ckv3","text":"CIP is active in all NEB restriction enzyme buffers","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a8v4t","text":"DNA purification is required before ligation","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5vu8b","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a 20 µL reaction as follows: A B 1 COMPONENT AMOUNT 2 DNA 1 pmol of DNA ends* 3 CutSmart ®   Buffer (10X) 2 μl 4 CIP 1 unit 5 H 2 O, purified  to 20 μl** * Note: 1 pmol of DNA ends is about 1 μg of a 3 kb plasmid. ** Scale larger reaction volumes proportionally. DNA 1 pmol of DNA ends* CutSmart® Buffer (10X) 2 μl CIP 1 unit H2O, purified to 20 μl**
Purify DNA by gel purification, spin-column ( NEB #T1020 or NEB #T1030 ) or phenol extraction.
creator: New England   Biolabs
